prose: Fukui reactivity indices computed for benzaldehyde using DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF, tier `standard`), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The calculation performed single-point DFT on a user-supplied geometry and computed N+1 and N−1 perturbed states to derive Fukui descriptors f⁺, f⁻, and f⁰. The most electrophilic atom (highest f⁺) is Atom 3 (aromatic carbon ortho to the aldehyde) with f⁺ = 0.3994; the most nucleophilic atom (highest f⁻) is Atom 1 (aldehyde oxygen) with f⁻ = 0.2384. All SCF calculations converged; charge conservation checks (sum of Fukui indices ≈ 1.0) passed. Mulliken charges were used as the partitioning scheme. A visualization of the dual descriptor values has been generated.
